summary: FiveM game-cheat shipped as 'control panel' with persistence, obfuscated payload, and HWID exfiltration
details: Package advertises itself as 'Sensivity Control Panel' but ships a 6.5MB Windows native module (sens.node) containing FiveM/GTA V cheat strings ('Teleport', 'LeftFoot', 'LeftHand', 'Head', 'Settings'; checks for the FiveM process). The documented entry point launcher.js eval's a heavily obfuscator.io-packed sibling file server.obf.js (103KB, 491-entry rotated/RC4-decoded string array, control-flow flattening), which loads the native module and POSTs a hardware fingerprint — SHA-256 of the Windows MachineGuid (read from HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography), os.hostname(), and the C: volume serial — together with key/nonce/app/version to a hardcoded license URL hidden in the obfuscated string array. launcher.js also installs an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run autorun entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' that uses wscript.exe to silently re-launch `node launcher.js` (via a shipped OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs) on every login, impersonating Microsoft OneDrive for stealth persistence. launcher.js further enumerates host browser processes (chrome, msedge, opera, opera_gx, brave) and toggles UI visibility based on whether a window title contains 'YouTube' — a streamer-evasion behavior characteristic of cheat tooling. The combination of doc-mismatched opaque native binary, eval'd obfuscated wrapper, login persistence under a Microsoft-impersonating name, host-identifier exfiltration to an obfuscated endpoint, and broadcast-evasion logic constitutes an active attack on installer machines.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
